title: Arrange2DDefinition Object
description: This object defines all of the settings associated with a 2D arrangement. This is used for both rectangular and true shape arrangements, but some properties are ignored in some cases.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: globalQuantity
properties.description: Returns the parameter that controls the global quantity. You can modify the value by using the properties on the returned ModelParameter object.

properties.name: globalRotation
properties.description: Gets and sets the global rotation type.

properties.name: grainDirection
properties.description: Defines the angle of the grain direction of the envelope. An angle of 0 is in the X direction of the envelope. You can modify the value by using the properties on the returned ModelParameter object.   This is only valid when the solver type is True Shape and it returns null in all other cases.

properties.name: isCreateCopies
properties.description: Gets if the original components were moved to create the arrangement or copied were created. This value can only be set when creating a new arrangement.

properties.name: isGlobalDirectionFaceUp
properties.description: Gets and sets the global direction for selected faces. When true, the components specified by selecting a face will be oriented such that the selection face will be oriented upward in the arrangement.

properties.name: isPartInPartAllowed
properties.description: Gets and sets if parts can be nested within void areas of other parts.   This is only used when the solver type is 2D True Shape and is ignored for 2D Rectangular solutions.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: solverType
properties.description: Gets the type of arrange feature defined by this definition.

classType.title: Arrange2DDefinition.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

globalQuantity.title: Arrange2DDefinition.globalQuantity Property
globalQuantity.description: Returns the parameter that controls the global quantity. You can modify the value by using the properties on the returned ModelParameter object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = arrange2DDefinition_var.globalQuantity

globalRotation.description: Gets and sets the global rotation type.

syntax.python:

# Set the value of the property.
